title: Unified stats/events layer as single source of truth for observability
context: Engineering memory system (Hunch) expanding beyond autoreview into telemetry; multiple surfaces (CLI, extension UI, LM tools, MCP) need consistent access to metrics. Touches invariant(s): con_03a0b94b2e (The agent hook must never block an edit on failure: any error or unrecognized input emits nothing and exits 0).
decision: Create core/events.ts and core/stats.ts modules implementing STOCK→RETURN→COMPOUNDING model: collect events into a log, compute aggregates (stats), and expose via standardized duration-query API (7d, 24h, etc.) shared across CLI command, home/console/stats extension views, and LM tools.
consequences: All observability flows through appendEvent/readEvents/computeStats APIs; no per-surface duplication
Duration parsing (parseSince) becomes standard across all query surfaces
Foundation for LM tools and MCP client to reason about engineering metrics as first-class data
Establishes telemetry as compounding value metric (receipt of what's accumulated vs what it caught)
alternatives_rejected: Per-surface stats collection (would fragment APIs, duplicate aggregation logic)
Embedding stats in autoreview/hookpolicy modules (couples observability to decision logic)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Per-surface stats collection (would fragment APIs, duplicate aggregation logic)
Embedding stats in autoreview/hookpolicy modules (couples observability to decision logic)
